headline: Gone in 60 minutes
summary: Legendary journalist Scott Pelley was reportedly fired from '60 Minutes'. This occurred after he questioned the installation of 'sycophants' in top CBS ranks. The article highlights editorial failures by Bari Weiss and Nick Bilton.

headline: The Sonos Era 100 speaker is down to its lowest price in months
summary: The Sonos Era 100 smart speaker is currently on sale for $189, a $30 discount. This marks its lowest price in months across multiple retailers. It's an ideal time to buy for new or existing Sonos setups.

headline: This is your laptop… on AI
summary: Big Tech companies are heavily pushing AI as a transformative force for personal computing. Nvidia's Jensen Huang described a completely new way of using laptops powered by AI. Developer conferences are focusing on AI's pervasive impact on how we interact with technology.

headline: What happens when your phone is confiscated at the airport
summary: US citizens and international travelers can have their phones confiscated by border agents at airports. This can happen even without wrongdoing, leaving travelers with little choice. The article highlights an incident where a labor organizer's phone was seized upon returning from Europe.

headline: Belkin's New Switch 2 Charging Grip Keeps Your Battery Charged
summary: Belkin has released a new charging grip for the Nintendo Switch 2. This accessory is designed to extend the console's battery life. It allows for longer gaming sessions on the go.

headline: Steam Store Updated With a Personal Calendar of New Games
summary: The Steam Store has introduced a new feature: a personal calendar. This calendar helps users track upcoming game releases. It provides a personalized overview of new games relevant to the user.

headline: Star Wars: The Acolyte Creator Would 'Absolutely' Come Back for Season 2 if Disney Greenlights It
summary: Leslye Headland, creator of Star Wars: The Acolyte, is eager for a second season. She confirmed her willingness to return if Disney approves. A second season for the show is currently unconfirmed.

headline: We Build LEGO Shrek, Donkey, and Puss in Boots, a Plastic Homage to the Classic DreamWorks Movies
summary: New LEGO sets featuring characters from the Shrek franchise are now available. These sets include Shrek, Donkey, and Puss in Boots. The reviewer found building them 'surprisingly graphic and tons of fun'.

headline: Rapist Paul Quinn's 24-year sentence an insult, says wrongly convicted Andrew Malkinson
summary: Paul Quinn was sentenced to 24 years for a 2003 rape, a crime for which Andrew Malkinson wrongly served 17 years. Malkinson expressed outrage, calling the sentence an 'insult' given his own unjust imprisonment. The case highlights severe miscarriages of justice.

headline: Downing Street hits out at 'people seeking to stir division' after Vance's Nowak post
summary: Downing Street criticized those 'seeking to stir division' following a post by the US vice president. The US VP had referenced Henry Nowak's death on X, stating the 'only response is righteous anger'. The statement indicates a diplomatic disagreement over the tone or content of the social media post.

headline: Buffy and Ted Lasso star Anthony Head dies at 72
summary: British actor Anthony Head has passed away at the age of 72. He was known for roles in 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer,' 'Ted Lasso,' 'Merlin,' and 'Little Britain'. His death was due to complications from pneumonia.

headline: Ex-Channel 4 News host Jon Snow reveals he has Alzheimer's
summary: Jon Snow, former lead presenter for Channel 4 News for 32 years, has revealed his Alzheimer's diagnosis. He will document his experience navigating the condition in a new film. The announcement sheds light on his personal health journey.
